Okay, Rule 8, always have available an arsenal of closes so you don't repeat yourself. You know this book, The Closer Survival Guide, which is what this segment is based on, has 126 closes. Now what we've done is in this module, we're going to build you a quick fix program where you can actually assess any of these 200 closes, because we've added some since this book, 200 closes so you can grab them quick, get a quick fix, get a quick solution and close the deal. If you don't have the close available, you're not going to know what to say. Most people quit because they lack variations of closes. You got three or four or five closes. They resist you three or four or five times. You're done. Look, you need a large variety to handle all the various customer types, all the different types of objections. Remember, correct estimation of effort. You don't go into a war without the correct estimation of people, artillery, food, supplies, etc. It is said that most prospects close after five attempts. I don't know if that's true or not, but I do know this. The average salesperson only has four closes available and I've been in front of hundreds of thousands of salespeople. Look, I don't want four or five closes. I want hundreds just in case. I don't want just enough food to eat this winter. I want extra just in case. You know what I'm saying? I don't prepare for a hurricane at a minimal level. I want to prepare for the hurricane at maximum level so that I am prepared. You want to become a master, a master at the close through repetition. I know when you watch us, you're like, yeah, I need to persist more. I need to hang in there more. I need to try harder. You can't if you don't have the technology and you don't know it. Those four or five I just gave you. By so many different industries, by so many different people and taught in so many different classes, they're not even effective anymore. Certainly they shouldn't be the only thing you depend on. Would you agree? Look, most salespeople are not aware of all the possibilities that they will need to close over.
